sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Set a 300 million ADA Net Change Limit for Epochs 563–635
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: The Cardano Constitution, at Article IV, Section 3, requires that before withdrawal of Treasury funds, the community must have established a Net Change Limit (NCL)., This means passing a NCL in a formal on-chain governance info action., The NCL represents the maximum amount of ada which may be withdrawn from the Cardano Treasury in a given period., We believe that the NCL covering the period from the start of epoch 563 to the end of epoch 635 should be 300M ADA.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Cardano Constitution, at Article IV, Section 3, requires that before withdrawal of Treasury funds, the community must have established a Net Change Limit (NCL).
Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): This means passing a NCL in a formal on-chain governance info action.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The NCL represents the maximum amount of ada which may be withdrawn from the Cardano Treasury in a given period.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, low materiality): We believe that the NCL covering the period from the start of epoch 563 to the end of epoch 635 should be 300M ADA.
Claim (other, supported_in_proposal, low materiality): Existing and pending NCLs only cover periods through the end of 2025.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): However, many current and upcoming budget proposals are structured around 12-month timelines, extending into 2026.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The Cardano Constitution, at Article IV, Section 3, requires that before withdrawal of Treasury funds, the community must have established a Net Change Limit (NCL)." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: weak controls, unclear delivery, or governance precedent could outweigh the benefit of InfoAction.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
